introSection: Soak'd (originally known as Slippery When Wet) was a steel suspended roller coaster that was formerly located at Freestyle Music Park in Myrtle Beach, South Carolina, USA.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
Slippery When Wet opened with Hard Rock Park in 2008. It was renamed to Soak'd in 2009.

After Freestyle Music Park's closure, the ride remained standing but not operating for several years before being relocated to Sun World Da Nang Downtown in Vietnam. It was rebuilt by early 2016 and was intended to operate under the name Highway Boat. However, the ride never opened and was demolished in June 2017 alongside Paradise Fall. Highway Boat was built in its place.

sections.title: Trains
sections.content: Single car trains. Riders are arranged 2 across in 2 rows for a total of 4 riders per car.

fullText:
Soak'd (originally known as Slippery When Wet) was a steel suspended roller coaster that was formerly located at Freestyle Music Park in Myrtle Beach, South Carolina, USA.

Slippery When Wet opened with Hard Rock Park in 2008. It was renamed to Soak'd in 2009.

After Freestyle Music Park's closure, the ride remained standing but not operating for several years before being relocated to Sun World Da Nang Downtown in Vietnam. It was rebuilt by early 2016 and was intended to operate under the name Highway Boat. However, the ride never opened and was demolished in June 2017 alongside Paradise Fall. Highway Boat was built in its place.



Single car trains. Riders are arranged 2 across in 2 rows for a total of 4 riders per car.
